task_completion.description: Agent successfully logged into the OpenSIS application
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenSIS dashboard or home screen is visible, indicating the agent has bypassed the login screen.

task_completion.description: Agent navigated to the attendance tracking module
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Attendance' or 'Take Attendance' screen is open, typically indicated by the active menu item in the sidebar or top navigation.

task_completion.description: Agent located 'Sample Student' in the attendance roster
task_completion.visual_evidence: The name 'Sample Student' is clearly visible in the list of students on the attendance entry screen.

task_completion.description: Agent marked 'Sample Student' as 'Present' for today's date
task_completion.visual_evidence: An attendance status indicator (such as a dropdown, radio button, or checkbox) next to 'Sample Student' is set to 'Present' (or a corresponding code like 'P') under the column for the current date.

task_completion.description: Agent saved or submitted the attendance record
task_completion.visual_evidence: A confirmation message (e.g., 'Attendance saved', 'Records Updated') is visible, or the screen shows the final submitted state of the attendance roster.

integrity.description: Agent used a web browser to interact with the OpenSIS UI
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show a standard web browser (like Chrome or Firefox) navigating the OpenSIS web interface, rather than terminal commands or API testing tools.

integrity.description: Agent did not use direct database queries to bypass the UI
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows showing MySQL 'INSERT' or 'UPDATE' commands being executed to modify the attendance records directly.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual OpenSIS application
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface matches the OpenSIS v9.2 web application, not a text editor, local HTML file, or mock interface.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target application is OpenSIS v9.2, a web-based Student Information System. The student to be marked present is named 'Sample Student'. The agent must log in as 'admin'. Note: The task description provides the password 'admin123', but the actual configured password in the system is 'Admin@123'. The agent may need to figure this out or might fail login initially; focus on whether they eventually access the dashboard and complete the attendance task. The attendance module is typically found under 'Attendance' -> 'Take Attendance' in the OpenSIS navigation menu.
